label: Italian top 100 (most common)
description: The 100 most frequent words in modern written and spoken Italian. Dominated by function words (articles, prepositions, conjunctions, pronouns), the highest-frequency verbs (essere, avere, fare, dire, andare, potere, dovere, volere, sapere, vedere), and a small set of ultra-common nouns (cosa, casa, anno, giorno, tempo, uomo, donna). A learner needs nearly all of these to begin functioning at A1.

label: Italian words 101-200
description: Words 101-200 by frequency. Still solidly A1/A2: common verbs of perception, motion and possession (guardare, aspettare, conoscere, chiamare, portare), high-frequency nouns of everyday life (vita, occhio, padre, madre, città, libro, parola), and core adjectives (piccolo, buono, primo, ultimo, vecchio).

label: Italian words 201-300
description: Words 201-300 by frequency. Expands into mid-A2 territory: more verbs of speech and cognition (chiedere, rispondere, ricordare, capire, scrivere), social and family lexis (figlio, ragazzo, gente, problema), and more specific adjectives (giovane, importante, possibile, difficile).

label: Italian words 301-400
description: Words 301-400 by frequency. Late A2 / early B1: state verbs and weather (sembrare, succedere, piovere), abstract concepts (mondo, storia, ragione, momento), and qualifying adjectives (certo, intero, libero, sicuro).

label: Italian words 401-500
description: Words 401-500. B1 core: more specific verbs (decidere, riuscire, restare, mancare), body and time lexis (mese, settimana, faccia, testa), and evaluative adjectives (forte, debole, simile, diverso).

label: Italian words 501-600
description: Words 501-600. Solid B1: civic and institutional lexis (governo, legge, paese, servizio), more particular verbs (offrire, raccontare, ottenere, riconoscere), and quantifying or degree adjectives (mezzo, doppio, pieno, vuoto).

label: Italian words 601-700
description: Words 601-700. B1 stretch / early B2: media and information lexis (notizia, articolo, immagine), low-frequency-but-useful verbs (svolgere, accadere, dipendere, occupare), and shades of meaning in adjectives (preciso, esatto, attuale, evidente).

label: Italian words 701-800
description: Words 701-800. B2 core: economic and political vocabulary (politica, economia, mercato, crisi), verbs of formal interaction (sostenere, dichiarare, presentare, garantire), and adjectives of evaluation (essenziale, opportuno, generale, profondo).

label: Italian words 801-900
description: Words 801-900. B2 / early C1: more academic and discursive lexis (concetto, principio, fattore, processo), reflexive and modal-flavoured verbs (concentrarsi, accorgersi, dedicarsi, intendere), and abstract adjectives (specifico, complesso, fondamentale, costante).

label: Italian words 901-1000
description: Words 901-1000. C1 territory at the high end, but still highly useful for B2+. Specialised vocabulary (analisi, ricerca, strumento, settore), low-frequency verbs that nonetheless recur in formal text (esercitare, costituire, rappresentare, verificare), and precise adjectives (rigoroso, accurato, deliberato, idoneo).

label: Italian words 1001-2000
description: Words 1001-2000 by merged-lemma frequency. Includes most basic function words demoted by source-merge methodology (il, di, e, che, non, a, in, per, con and most prepositions/conjunctions/articles got ranks here due to their absence from the ItWaC POS-tagged lemma list and NVdB's flat tier ranking). Also genuine 1001-2000 lemmas: vocabulary for refined writing, less-frequent verbs, regional and topic-specific words.

label: Italian words 2001-5000
description: Words 2001-5000. C1/C2 territory. After the re-rank pass, this band picked up function words (because of the source-merge artefact described above) and a long tail of less-common but useful lemmas (specific food items, regional places, technical vocabulary).

label: Italian words 5001+
description: Words beyond rank 5000 in the merged lemma list. Picked up after the re-rank pass: highly specific vocabulary, low-frequency reflexive verbs (innamorarsi, arrabbiarsi), and edge-case lemmas. C2 territory or beyond.
